name: Herb Nut Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon minced fresh chives (or green onion tops)
1 tablespoon minced fresh parsley (OR 1 teaspoon dried parsley)
1 cup Water heated to 120 to 130 degrees
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine filling ingredients; mix well and refrigerate.
2. Grease a 13 x 9 inches pan.
3. In a large bowl, combine flour mixture with yeast from foil packet, pine nuts, parsley, basil, tarragon, and garlic powder; mix well.
4. Stir in hot water and butter until the dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
5. Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface. With greased or floured hands, shape dough into a ball.
6. Knead dough 5 minutes until smooth, sprinkling with additional flour if necessary to reduce stickiness.
7. Cover dough with large bowl; let rest for 5 minutes.
8. Divide cream cheese mixture into 12 equal pieces; flatten each to a 3 inches circle.
9. Place cheese piece on the center of each circle.
10. Wrap dough around cheese, pressing edges of dough to seal.
11. Place rolls, seam side down, in greased pan.
12. Cover loosely with greased plastic wrap and cloth towel.
13. Let rise in warm place for 30 minutes or until doubled in size.
14. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
15. Uncover dough; in a small bowl, beat egg white and milk until well blended.
16. Brush over rolls. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
17. Bake for 20 to 30 minutes or until golden brown. Serve warm.
